data.title: Five9 falls on weaker guidance, receives downgrade
data.description:
Five9 (NASDAQ:FIVN) plunged following second quarter 2024 financial results that surpassed expectations, but featured a cut to its annual revenue guidance due to softer booking trends.

Five9 shares tumbled 25% during early market action on Friday.

Baird downgraded the stock to Neutral from Outperform due to growth concerns and reduced visibility, and drastically reduced its price target to $40 from $90.

Needham also lowered its price target to $48 from $90, while Piper Sandler decreased its price target to $47 from $58.

"While downside could be somewhat limited by valuation, we expect reduced visibility and ongoing competitive threats to be significant overhangs," said Baird analyst William Power, in an investor note.

"2024 growth now guided to 11.5% vs. 15.9% previously, suggesting reduced visibility," Power added. "That also implies subscription revenue growth below other industry leaders, raising competitive question."

Baird also highlighted increasing competition in the cloud contact center, from providers such as Zoom (ZM), Microsoft (MSFT), Google (GOOG)(GOOGL), Amazon (AMZN) along with a handful of private companies.

"As with other applications that are rapidly moving to the cloud, contact center is rapidly being virtualized as companies look to upgrade legacy contact center platforms and/or are looking for contact center solutions to integrate with CRM cloud overhauls," Power noted.

Five9 is a contact center as a service provider that replaces older contact center phone systems with a cloud, software-based platform.

Five9 has a Buy rating from both Seeking Alpha analysts and Wall Street analysts. However, it has a Hold rating from Seeking Alpha's Quant system, which routinely beats the market.

data.title: Akamai surges after analysts remain largely positive following strong Q2 results
data.description:
• Akamai Technologies' (NASDAQ:AKAM) stock soared about 10% on Friday following second quarter results which drew positive reactions from analysts.
  • Raymond James maintained its Outperform rating and $115 price target on the shares but raised its estimates following the second quarter results.
  • Analysts led by Frank G. Louthan IV noted that Akamai's management posted its first quarter with a majority of revenue coming from security, signaling a notable inflection point for the company. Two-thirds of revenue now comes from compute and security, and they believe management is executing well in both arenas, offsetting macro headwinds in delivery.
  • The company's management mentioned on the call that while lower traffic growth and general retail and gaming weakness are pressuring delivery. Akamai is not seeing customers substituting for lower cost providers. This contrasts with what peer Fastly (FSLY) indicated, the analysts added. Macro decision-making remains elongated, too, similar to other macro slowdown commentary the analysts have heard elsewhere in their coverage.
  • The analysts stated that Akamai is seeing strength in compute and security driven by the investments in both. The company's management is seeing strong early adoption of its new cloud offering, underscored by their own adoption and migration of traditional clouds.
  • They expect Akamai to drive upside to margins over time from these more profitable areas of the business. They think Guardicore in particular is paying dividends as the company now has a unified solution for micro-segmentation, Zero Trust Network access, firewall, and threat hunting among other services. The analysts believe that Selling Guardicore via the channel is adding significant value for partners as well.
  • In addition, the analysts think that there are no capital constraints on the company's ability to keep investing in the development of compute, and they expect Akamai's management to continue repurchasing shares and opportunistically considering M&A. Management raised the 2024 EPS guidance and is advancing toward their near-term operating margin target of about 30%.
  • Meanwhile, RBC Capital Markets said it was maintaining its Sector Perform rating on Akamai's stock and raising its price target to $100 from $92, after a "solid quarter."
  • Analysts led by Rishi Jaluria stated that Akamai reported solid results, and slightly raised guidance. Security and compute segments now contribute two-thirds of total revenue, as Delivery stabilized but remains challenged.
  • The analysts were encouraged by enterprise Compute traction, but Security and 2024 revenue guidance were underwhelming. On a positive note, margins/EPS moved higher.
  • The analysts think the risk/reward is balanced.
  • Oppenheimer kept a Perform rating on Akamai noting that it was a solid second quarter, and that cloud compute gaining traction, however, growth has likely bottomed.
  • Timothy Horan said that Akamai reported strong second quarter compute growth and increased its compute guidance by 200 bps for 2024. Oppenheimer have increased its modeled compute growth rates accordingly.
  • Weakness in delivery dragged down results, as expected. The high level of demand for cloud compute, its customer relationships and strong capital expenditure spend should accelerate growth. The company is seeing strong wins in new customers for cloud, but legacy Media customers are still the major enterprise cloud customers, according to the analyst.
  • Horan added that Noname, which was acquired by Akamai, is seeing strong upselling. Akamai guided to a slowdown in the second half. Oppenheimer remains Perform on delivery weakness but is increasingly bullish on compute.
  • Negatively, the third quarter is guided to 4% revenue growth, plus higher capital expenditure and flat earnings, the analyst added.
  • Earlier on Friday, Craig-Hallum had upgraded the stock to Buy.
  • Akamai Technologies' (AKAM) has a Hold rating at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating system, which consistently beats the market. Meanwhile, the Seeking Alpha authors' (2 authors in total) average rating is more positive with a Buy, and so is the average Wall Street analysts' rating, Buy.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, and Dow reverse course and yields also inch lower
data.description:
Major market averages fell on Friday, a day after investors found some comfort from a bigger-than-expected decline in weekly initial jobless claims.

Early on and the S&P 500 (SP500) was -0.1%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.1%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) down 7 basis points to at 3.92%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 3 basis points to 4.01%.

Major U.S. stock indexes also ended higher on Thursday, as worries about the economy sliding into a recession were eased after the initial jobless claims came in less than expected.

"Barely a week since recessionary concerns rocked markets, investors have regained some poise, with share prices largely recouping the steep losses of the last few days," Richard Hunter, Interactive Investor said.

"Markets are closing out the week in a Disney paradise after having starting the week completely frozen! The buoyant market mood over the past 24 hours was given a significant boost by a decent U.S. jobless claims report," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

The better outturn pointed to enough strength in the U.S. labor market not to suggest that it is not falling apart. It certainly provided some evidence to the view that part of the weak payrolls may have been weather-related effects, Reid added.

Traders were focused on the initial jobless claims report, as they looked to gauge the labor market after last week's disappointing jobs report.

The economic calendar is light today, with only the Baker-Hughes rig count on tap.

data.title: News Corp. evaluating potential sale of Australian TV business Foxtel
data.description:
News Corp. (NASDAQ:NWSA) is considering potential options for its Australian pay-TV and streaming business Foxtel after getting some takeover interest. News Corp rose 1.4% on Friday.

“We had no imminent intent to sell Foxtel, but are reviewing potential strategic and financial options for the business with our advisers and engaging with third-parties in light of that external interest,” News Corp CEO Robert Thomson said on the company's Q2 earnings call on Thursday.

Thomson described the interest in Foxtel as a "significant overture."

"We have had an overture a significant overdue, which we're naturally assessing -- but let me make absolutely clear that we have full faith in the potential of Foxtel and our talented team at Foxtel who've fashioned a world-class viewing experience and created Kayo, which really is the best at streaming platform globally, hence the interest in emulating its success in the U.S," Thomson added.

News Corp. (NWSA) owns about 65% of Foxtel, while Telstra Group (OTCPK:TLGPY) owns the remainder.

As of June 30, Foxtel's total closing paid subscribers were nearly 4.7M a 1% increase compared to the prior year, News Corp disclosed in its Q2 results on Thursday.
